steps.title: Follow the scholars' logic
steps.content: The scholars argue that the Harlem Renaissance never really ended because it continues to shape culture in the United States today. Their conclusion depends on treating continuing influence as a way for a cultural movement to continue.

steps.title: Reject unrelated principles
steps.content: Choice A discusses whether literary and nonliterary innovations make a movement influential. Choice B says innovation makes a movement unlikely to last, which conflicts with the argument. Choice C introduces a literary versus nonliterary distinction that the conclusion does not require.

steps.title: State the continuity link
steps.content: The remaining choice supplies the needed principle: if a movement's influence continues, then in some sense the movement itself continues. The answer is D.
